action.content: Can you write a poem about the pros and cons of playing a lot of video games? Please make sure it's at least 40 sentences long (don't forget to add punctuations). You must highlight some words or phrases in your response, like *highlighted phrase*.
action.label: context

action.content:
The screen glows bright, a portal to new worlds,  
Where heroes rise and ancient dragons swirl.  
I press a button, and the game begins,  
With pixel hearts and digital designs.  

At first, it feels like magic in my hand,  
A place where time can pause and stand.  
I solve a puzzle, defeat a foe,  
And watch the score begin to grow.  

But hours slip away like water through a sieve,  
While chores and duties quietly leave.  
The room grows dim, the sun goes down,  
And I am lost within the game's domain.  

My eyes grow tired, my neck begins to ache,  
A silent toll that only I can take.  
The chair is hard, the floor is cold,  
And stories of the real world grow old.  

Yet in that world, I find a friend,  
Who shares the same adventure, same end.  
We laugh together at a funny glitch,  
And feel a bond that feels quite rich.  

It teaches me strategy, patience, and skill,  
To think ahead and make me think.  
A level hard, I try again and again,  
Until I finally win, a champion then.  

But when the battery fades and power dies,  
I feel a hollow ache inside my eyes.  
The silence hits, the room feels vast,  
And I am left to face the past.  

I miss the sun, the wind, the rain,  
The simple joys that nature gains.  
My hands are numb, my mind is slow,  
From staring at the glowing glow.  

The pros are clear: a world of wonder,  
Where courage and adventure are forever.  
The cons are deep: a life unspent,  
With time and moments that were meant.  

I could have walked, I could have talked,  
I could have felt the grass and stalk.  
Instead, I sat and pressed a key,  
And let the hours pass so free.  

But then I think of all the fun,  
The battles won, the levels run.  
The friendships made, the laughs we shared,  
The joy that games can bring and bear.  

Perhaps it's balance that we need,  
To let the games and life proceed.  
To play a bit, then step away,  
And find the light of day.  

So here's my plea, to all who play,  
Don't let the screen consume your day.  
Enjoy the thrill, the challenge, the fight,  
But don't lose sight of what's right.  

For in the end, the real world waits,  
With sun and stars and open gates.  
And though the game may seem so grand,  
It's life that truly holds the hand.  

So put the controller down, take a breath,  
And let the real adventure begin.  
The world is vast, the story long,  
And you are more than just a player strong.  

The pros are sweet, the cons are true,  
But life is what we choose to do.  
So play with purpose, play with care,  
And leave a little space for air.  

For every level, every quest,  
Has a cost that we must test.  
And when the game is done, we face,  
The quiet moments, time and space.  

So let us play, but play with grace,  
And make the most of every place.  
For in the balance, we will find,  
The joy that games and life can bind.  

The screen may glow, but life is real,  
With stories that are hard to steal.  
So take a break, look up, and see,  
The world is waiting for you, free.  

And when you return, with fresh and clear,  
The game will wait, as always near.  
But life, my friend, is not a game,  
It's a journey, not the same.  

So cherish both, the virtual and the true,  
The pixel world and the world of you.  
For in the mix, we find our way,  
Through play and life, to end of day.  

The pros are many, the cons are few,  
But balance is the key to view.  
So play your game, but don't forget,  
The real world is where you're set.  

And when the credits roll, and all is done,  
You'll know you've lived, and not just won.  
For in the end, the greatest prize,  
Is living life, with open eyes.  

So here's to games, both good and bad,  
The lessons learned, the paths we had.  
And here's to life, with all its cost,  
The greatest game, the one we host.  

Play on, but remember, too,  
The world is waiting for you.  
The sun will rise, the stars will shine,  
And life will be, and life will be fine.  

So let the games be part of you,  
But not the whole, not all, not through.  
For in the balance, we will find,  
The joy that games and life can bind.  

And when the screen goes dark and still,  
You'll know you've lived, and not just filled.  
With moments real, and memories true,  
That last forever, bright and new.  

So play with heart, but play with care,  
And let the real world be there.  
For in the end, the greatest prize,  
Is living life, with open eyes.  

The pros are sweet, the cons are deep,  
But life is what we choose to keep.  
So play your game, but don't forget,  
The real world is where you're set.  

And when the credits roll, and all is done,  
You'll know you've lived, and not just won.  
For in the end, the greatest prize,  
Is living life, with open eyes.  

So here's to games, both good and bad,  
The lessons learned, the paths we had.  
And here's to life, with all its cost,  
The greatest game, the one we host.  

Play on, but remember, too,  
The world is waiting for you.  
The sun will rise, the stars will shine,  
And life will be, and life will be fine.  

So let the games be part of you,  
But not the whole, not all, not through.  
For in the balance, we will find,  
The joy that games and life can bind.  

And when the screen goes dark and still,  
You'll know you've lived, and not just filled.  
With moments real, and memories true,  
That last forever, bright and new.  

So play with heart, but play with care,  
And let the real world be there.  
For in the end, the greatest prize,  
Is living life, with open eyes.  

The pros are sweet, the cons are deep,  
But life is what we choose to keep.  
So play your game, but don't forget,  
The real world is where you're set.  

And when the credits roll, and all is done,  
You'll know you've lived, and not just won.  
For in the end, the greatest prize,  
Is living life, with open eyes.
action.label: response
